author: Pam Baker
tokens_note: All sub-agents ran via general-purpose dispatcher (Sonnet); tokens are subagent_tokens from <usage> tails
reader_iter1_root_cause: Assembler leaked entire article body before <html> tag due to malformed self-documenting comment in skeleton.html (inner '-->' markers closed outer comment prematurely)
reader_lowest_dim_note: Body is 100% raw <blockquote class='anchor'> stacks with no Editor connective prose (consequence of 0% bridge ratio); reads as quote tapestry
notes: First full-EPUB build (~400 pages, 109 Calibre-fragmented splits). Iteration 2 forced by Assembler-skeleton contract bug (root cause logged); fix was stripping doctype-to-<html> preamble before substitution. Editor delivered deepest verbatim retention logged to date (86.1%, 0% bridges).

author: Ethan Mollick
tokens_note: Editor+Proofreader+Reader only; Designer usage not exposed
notes: First ai_topical build under v2.1 category-aware upgrade; Editor scored on AI rubric column; iter2 due to reworked bridge u_0002; Designer interrupted by session limit after writing complete verified HTML

author: Morgan Housel
tokens_note: All sub-agents ran via general-purpose dispatcher; tokens are subagent_tokens from <usage> tails. Includes Editor's 3 internal iterations + Reader's 2 iterations.
reader_iter1_root_cause: Two failures stacked: (1) Assembler illustration-lookup bug — Designer keyed illustration_plan by source_id ('ch1§1¶0_h') while Assembler matched by auto-generated unit_id ('u_0000'); all 22 diagrams rendered to zero figures. (2) Editor's iter-1 anchor threshold of ≥7 + bridge-drop in iter-2 (boilerplate) produced a 30%-coverage Kindle-highlights export — anecdote setups (Berggruen, Read, Fuscone) were dropped, leaving punchlines orphaned.
reader_lowest_dim_note: No in-text .xref instances; cross-chapter references require TOC scroll-back. CSS hook is defined but never instantiated.
notes: Self-help × deep × graphic_heavy × A_only. Editor's first bridge attempt shipped 251 identical templated stubs ('The argument continues in this direction.') — pure boilerplate that passed the Proofreader's factual gate but defeated the spec's intent. Iter-2 dropped them entirely (anchor-only). Reader iter-1 failed at 30% coverage → iter-3 Editor lowered threshold to ≥5 preserving narrative-setup paragraphs adjacent to anchored anecdotes. Final: 836 anchors, 71% verbatim source coverage, 22 chapter-specific diagrams (17 Mermaid + 5 SVG), 8 pull-quotes. Comprehension test: 10/10 correct.

author: Eckhart Tolle
wall_clock_note: Dominated by Designer duration_ms (9,833,664 ms / 163.9 min) which reflects dispatcher wall-time including waits, not pure compute; other personas sum to ~29 min.
tokens_note: All sub-agents via general-purpose dispatcher; tokens are subagent_tokens from <usage> tails (not split input/output). Model attribution: Editor/Designer/Reader=Opus, Proofreader/QuestionGenerator=Sonnet.
estimated_cost_usd: ~$11-12 (rough; subagent_tokens not split in/out; Opus 390k + Sonnet 193k at blended rates)
reader_lowest_dim_note: Body is largely back-to-back verbatim anchor blockquotes (143 anchors, 10 bridges = 0.79% bridge ratio); reads as a 'quote wall' rather than flowing condensed prose, holding visual_pleasure and finish-rate at 4 not 5.
notes: First single-pass PASS since Atomic Habits (no rework). Pirate-EPUB ad chapters (10 Chinese promo pages, chenjin5.com) correctly excluded by the ad_chapter* naming convention. Dialogue-form spiritual self-help; 143 verbatim anchors char-exact (Proofreader 0 violations on 72-anchor stratified sample). 7 hand-authored inline-SVG concept diagrams (one per concept chapter [1,2,3,5,6,7,10]) + 7 frozen-seed watercolor openers, all 7 successfully base64-inlined by the Assembler (0 fallbacks) -> fully self-contained 780KB HTML. Comprehension 10/10.

book_title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
wall_clock_note: Dominated by Proofreader iter-1 duration_ms (14,040,429 ms / 234 min), an outlier reflecting a slow rapidfuzz install / network stall in the dispatcher, not compute. Excluding that span, all other personas sum to ~64 min. Treat wall as elapsed, not CPU.
tokens_note: All sub-agents via general-purpose dispatcher; tokens are subagent_tokens from <usage> tails (not split input/output). Model attribution: Editor/Designer/Reader=Opus, Ingestor/Proofreader/QuestionGenerator=Sonnet. Assembler is deterministic (Python, 0 LLM tokens).
estimated_cost_usd: ~$11-13 (rough; subagent_tokens not split in/out; Opus 388k + Sonnet 322k at blended rates)
reader_iter1_note: Proofreader gate failed iter-1 (pre-Reader) on 6 bridge_unsupported_claim violations; routed to Editor for scoped rework before the Reader ran.
reader_lowest_dim_note: Clears the >=4 terminal bar but is the weakest dim: long anecdotes (Onoda, Mustaine, the cliff-jump) compressed to one-line bridges, so thesis survives but emotional texture that makes the original stick is lost. Reader also flagged .xref hover-preview popovers + sub-chapter TOC anchors specified in the chrome contract are not wired (same navigation gap as Row 4) though navigation still scored 4.
notes: Second proofreader-fail-on-bridges build (after Row 4's templated-stub case, a different failure mode). Here the Editor's iter-1 bridges injected the third-person name 'Manson' into 4 first-person memoir spans (the source is 'I' throughout) plus one quote pulled from an uncited paragraph (ch7§4¶4) and one unsupported 'absolute freedom...hollow' phrasing. Scoped iter-2 Editor rework fixed exactly those 4 bridges (added ch7§4¶4 as a citation, stripped the name, regrounded the phrasing); iter-2 Proofreader: 205/205 anchors verbatim, 0 violations. First build to land in the 3-8% healthy bridge band recommended by Row 5's evaluation (3.34%) - and the quote-wall critique was correspondingly milder (visual_pleasure 4, finish 4, lowest dim is finish-rate-on-texture, not the raw 'quote wall' itself). 9 hand-authored inline-SVG concept diagrams (one per chapter, dark-mode-safe via CSS vars) + 9 frozen-seed hand_drawn_cartoon chapter openers, all 9 base64-inlined by the Assembler (0 fallbacks) -> fully self-contained 1.37MB HTML. Comprehension 9.5/10 (Q3 partial).

author: George Orwell
wall_clock_note: Cumulative span compute. Four editors ran concurrently and two illustrators ran concurrently, so true elapsed wall was well under this sum. Proofreader span uses self-reported 127s (harness duration_ms was an idle-inflated outlier).
tokens_note: Authoritative subagent_tokens from <usage> tails (not split in/out). Opus: editors+designer+illustrators+reader; Sonnet: proofreader+question_generator. Excludes orchestrator tokens.
notes: First FICTION build in the log (rows 1-6 were self-help/ai_topical). Single iteration, no Reader-gate rework. Editor split into 4 concurrent part-editors (Part I/II/III/Appendix) to defeat end-of-book thinning (Iron Law #7); per-chapter coverage held in-band except 3 deliberately over-retained chapters (ch21 O'Brien power doctrine 1.45x, ch23 'Do it to Julia!' 1.37x, ch25 Newspeak appendix 1.49x) documented as accepted_anomalies. Deterministic anchor verifier: 265/265 verbatim char-for-char, 0 mismatches. Proofreader caught 1 bridge hallucination (u_0080 'aged' not in cited spans) -> fixed in place, not a full rework loop. CHANNEL B PAYWALLED: Pollinations returned HTTP 402 on all 12 fetches; per user choice the 12 atmospheric images were hand-drawn as inline noir SVG by 2 concurrent Illustrator agents (counts as self-contained Channel A; 0 external assets). Reader 5/5 books_finished_rate, 10/10 comprehension, lowest dim illustration=4. Fully self-contained 310KB HTML, no CDN (Mermaid diagrams hand-rendered as inline SVG).

author: Daniel Kahneman
wall_clock_note: Real-elapsed estimate. 8 concurrent part-editors had idle-inflated duration_ms (g2/g6/g8 each self-reported 10.6-15.6M ms = mostly harness idle wait under concurrency, not compute); reliable non-editor spans (proofreader x2, designer, reader, question-gen, editor-rework) sum to ~24.9 min. True elapsed ~50 min.
tokens_note: Authoritative subagent_tokens from <usage> tails (not split in/out). Opus: 8 part-editors + bridge-rework + Designer + Reader; Sonnet: Proofreader x2 + QuestionGenerator. Ingestor/Assembler deterministic Python (0 LLM tokens). Largest build in the log (165k-word source).
estimated_cost_usd: ~$40-45 (rough; subagent_tokens not split in/out; Opus 1.65M + Sonnet 165k at blended rates)
retention_method: proportional-target (~27% of source); deep expository band
reader_iter1_note: Proofreader gate failed iter-1 (pre-Reader) on 12 bridge_unsupported_claim violations; scoped Editor bridge-rework (6 citation-widen / 6 rewrite) + 1 orchestrator citation-widen resolved all; Reader ran once, passed.
reader_lowest_dim_note: Quote-heavy rhythm (361 anchors, 5.12% bridge) reads as curated verbatim Kahneman rather than flowing distillation, and the source EPUBs preserved OCR garble snags the eye inside otherwise-clean quotes (kept verbatim per user instruction). Held visual_pleasure + finish-rate at 4.
notes: First expository_nonfic build in the log (rows 1-7: self_help/ai_topical/fiction). Source = a full 165k-word, 38-chapter classic. Generic ingest_epub.py mis-segmented this calibre EPUB (stale navMap #filepos anchors + identical <title> heads), so a TFAS-specific ingester segmented by embedded <h2> chapter headings; ch16 recovered from a corrupted calibre tag via regex fallback (no silent chapter drop). Editor fanned out to 8 concurrent part-editors over the 5 Parts to hold per-chapter coverage (all 40 sections in-band). SOURCE OCR CORRUPTION (~0.2% of tokens, e.g. "Jto Aad Jto", "Fischh off"): user chose ship-strictly-verbatim, so anchors preserve garble; disclosed in the Design Note + coverage.json. Deterministic anchor verifier: 361/361 char-exact. Proofreader bridge audit caught 12 hallucinations -> scoped rework, 0 residual. 20 self-contained inline-SVG/HTML-table diagrams (value function, fourfold 2x2, System 1/2, Linda Venn, regression, peak-end), no CDN/Mermaid. Appendices A/B + Notes + Index intentionally out of scope. Comprehension 10/10; Reader 4/5 terminal PASS.

book_title: Leadership and the One Minute Manager
author: Ken Blanchard, Patricia Zigarmi, Drea Zigarmi
wall_clock_note: Cumulative span compute; sequential single-chain pipeline, no concurrent fan-out. True elapsed ~31 min including intake beats and wakeup overhead.
tokens_note: Authoritative subagent_tokens from <usage> tails (not split in/out). Opus: Editor/Designer/Reader. Sonnet: Proofreader. Ingestor/Assembler deterministic (0 LLM tokens). A second Editor agent (210,967 tokens) was dispatched in error and ignored; excluded from total.
estimated_cost_usd: ~$16 (rough; Opus 370k + Sonnet 175k subagent_tokens not split in/out)
proofreader_iter1_violation_detail: u_0030 verbatim_quote had spurious leading double-quote not present in source; bridge_ratio measured 0.16 vs 0.10 ceiling (denominator ambiguity: Proofreader divided bridge words by anchor words rather than total words)
reader_lowest_dim_note: Thin chapters with one bridge + one anchor read as slide outline rather than narrative flow; sec-2 and sec-17 flagged as too sparse for their headings.
notes: Smallest source in the log by measured word count (16,240 words). SLII parable: 3 skills, 4 D-levels, 4 S-styles, matching rule, 3-part performance loop, 6 conversation types all present in verbatim anchor language. 6% retention (950/16,240) is the lowest coverage pct in the log but correct: narrative scaffolding (dialogue, story beats) scores <=0 on self_help rubric. 8 Mermaid diagrams carry structural load: S1-S4 quadrant, D1-D4 progression with regression arrows, D-to-S matching map, SMART flow, performance-management loop, six-conversations tree. Iter-1 Proofreader fail on simultaneous verbatim-metadata error + bridge_ratio denominator ambiguity; iter-2 Editor targeted rework (42s, 37k tokens) resolved both. Reader 5/5 illustration (strongest dim), 4/5 books_finished_rate (PASS), 9.5/10 comprehension.

author: David Brooks

Stale-draft race caught (first proofreader audited a mid-write 84-anchor draft; final 85). Session-limit pause resumed cleanly. Designer: linear, 8 inline-SVG diagrams, Channel A only, offline. author: Andrew Ross Sorkin

Prior session Ingestor+Editor(7 shards+rebalance)+Proofreader-iter1 ran in a separate session that died mid-rework; their telemetry was not captured across the session boundary. True end-to-end elapsed spans two sittings hours apart.
tokens_note: subagent_tokens from <usage> tails (not split in/out), THIS session only. Excludes uninstrumented prior-session Editor + Proofreader-iter1. Reader=Opus; Proofreader/Editor-rework/Designer=Sonnet; QuestionGenerator dispatched via general-purpose (Haiku-intended).
estimated_cost_usd: ~-9 (rough; subagent_tokens not split in/out; Opus 129k + Sonnet/other 384k at blended rates; excludes uninstrumented prior-session Editor)
proofread_history: iter1 (prior session): 27 bridge_unsupported_claim FAIL -> Editor rework (15 bridges); iter2 full re-audit (resume): 65/71 clean, 6 minor ungrounded specifics FAIL -> scoped Editor rework (5 citation-widen + 1 rewrite); re-audit: 6/6 grounded PASS. Final fabricated/ungrounded bridges: 0.
reader_lowest_dim_note: Verbatim-anchor-heavy (bridge ratio 4.16%, ~90%+ of body is stacked blockquotes joined by thin one-line bridges); reads as a high-quality excerpt anthology rather than fully propulsive narrative. Middle views (The Confidence Men, The Reckoning) can induce mild sourcebook fatigue. Clears the >=4 gate; comprehension is perfect.
notes: RESUMED build: a prior same-day session got through Ingest+Editor+bridge-rework but died before Proofreader re-verification (no HTML/verdict/run-log row). Resume reused the deterministic corpus (113,338 body words, 46 dated chapters, Feb 1929->June 1933) + the reworked edit_pack, then re-gated from scratch: deterministic anchor sweep 551/551 verbatim, fresh full Proofreader on all 71 bridges (6 minor ungrounded specifics -> scoped fix, 5 of 6 resolved by citation-widen against text already in source = original edit was sound but under-cited), re-audit PASS. Designer: 8 narrative views (Prologue / The Confidence Men / The Bull Runs Wild / Cracks in the Facade / Crash Week / The Reckoning / Pecora and the New Deal / Afterlives), 9 verbatim pull-quotes, ONE inline-SVG dark-mode-safe hero timeline (1929->1933 arc), Channel A only. Assembler: chaptered reader; fixed 2 shard-merge duplicate anchors (Reader-flagged) + added carry-forward chapter assignment so 5 empty-source pure-transition bridges were not silently dropped. Reader source-blind 10/10 comprehension, PASS 4/5 (books_finished_rate); would_skip_original true. Real EPUB-embedded cover extracted to books/covers/1929.jpg (OpenLibrary had no cover for ISBN 9780593296974).

author: Bessel van der Kolk
wall_clock_note: Cumulative instrumented span compute (~168.8 min). Heavily concurrent: 5 Editor shards ran in parallel and Designer∥QuestionGenerator concurrently, so true elapsed is far shorter. Includes 2 timed-out shards' wall + 4 recovery shards + 2 targeted revisions.
tokens_note: Sum of subagent_tokens <usage> tails (not split in/out). LOWER BOUND: shards A/B/D died mid-response so their final-turn token tails (621/102/207) drastically underreport their true multi-chapter edit cost; real total likely ~1.6M.
estimated_cost_usd: ~$30-40 (rough; Opus-heavy — editor/designer/reader on Opus, proofreader Sonnet, QG Haiku; subagent_tokens not split in/out; underreported per tokens_note)
reader_verdict: PASS — visual_pleasure 5, navigation 5, comprehension 5 (10/10), illustration 5, books_finished_rate 5; would_skip_original true
notes: 5-shard Editor sharding; shards B & D died mid-response (API connection closed) writing only ch5/ch14, recovered via 4 smaller gap-shards + 2 targeted band revisions (ch5 contract, ch8 expand). ch2/ch3/ch16 sit marginally under the strict expository 0.80 floor (0.75-0.79) but pass the general 70-130% law. 3 anchor sweep fails were corpus footnote-numeral false-positives (cleared). 1 bridge citation-widen. Shipped assemble.py was stale; v3.0 assembler authored.
